content.text:
Here are the verification results:

**1. Huang et al., "Compression Represents Intelligence Linearly", COLM 2024** -- OK. Title, authors, venue, year all correct.

**2. Joshi et al., "Personas as a Way to Model Truthfulness in Language Models", EMNLP 2024** -- DISCREPANCY: First author's name is **Nitish Joshi**, not "Nitarshan Joshi". All other details correct.

**3. Kadavath et al., "Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know", arXiv:2207.05221, 2022** -- OK. Title, authors (Kadavath, Conerly, Askell et al.), arXiv ID, and year all correct. Note: this remained an arXiv preprint (not published at a peer-reviewed venue).

**4. Kalai & Vempala, "Calibrated Language Models Must Hallucinate", STOC 2024** -- OK. Title, authors, venue (56th ACM STOC), year all correct.

**5. Kandpal et al., "Large Language Models Struggle to Learn Long-Tail Knowledge", ICML 2023** -- OK. Title, all five authors, venue, year all correct.

**6. Longpre et al., "Entity-Based Knowledge Conflicts in Question Answering", EMNLP 2021** -- OK. Title, all six authors, venue, year all correct.

**7. Li et al., "Formality is Favored: Unraveling the Learning Preferences of Large Language Models on Data with Conflicting Knowledge", EMNLP 2024** -- OK. Title, authors (Li, Cao, Huang, Chen), venue, year all correct.

**8. Li et al., "Emergent World Representations: Exploring a Sequence Model Trained on a Synthetic Task", ICLR 2023** -- MINOR DISCREPANCY: The middle initial of Fernanda Viégas is **B.** (Fernanda **B.** Viégas) in the official publication, but this is a minor formatting difference that references commonly omit. Otherwise title, authors, venue, year all correct.

**9. Li et al., "Inference-Time Intervention: Eliciting Truthful Answers from a Language Model", NeurIPS 2023** -- OK. Title, authors (Li, Patel, Viégas, Pfister, Wattenberg), venue, year all correct. Note: your listing says "Viegas" without the accent -- the correct spelling is "Viégas" but this is a diacritic issue, not a factual error.

**10. Liu et al., "Grokking as Compression: A Nonlinear Complexity Perspective", arXiv:2310.05918, 2023** -- MINOR NOTE: This was also presented at the **NeurIPS 2023 UniReps workshop**, but listing it as an arXiv preprint is not wrong. Title, authors (Liu, Zhong, Tegmark), arXiv ID, year all correct.

---

**Summary of issues to fix:**
- **Reference 2**: Change "Nitarshan Joshi" to **"Nitish Joshi"**.
- All other references are correct.

Sources:
- [COLM 2024 - Compression Represents Intelligence Linearly](https://openreview.net/forum?id=SHMj84U5SH)
- [ACL Anthology - Personas as a Way to Model Truthfulness](https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-main.364/)
- [arXiv - Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know](https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.05221)
- [ACM DL - Calibrated Language Models Must Hallucinate](https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3618260.3649777)
- [ICML - Large Language Models Struggle to Learn Long-Tail Knowledge](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v202/kandpal23a/kandpal23a.pdf)
- [ACL Anthology - Entity-Based Knowledge Conflicts](https://aclanthology.org/2021.emnlp-main.565/)
- [ACL Anthology - Formality is Favored](https://aclanthology.org/2024.emnlp-main.304/)
- [OpenReview - Emergent World Representations](https://openreview.net/forum?id=DeG07_TcZvT)
- [NeurIPS 2023 - Inference-Time Intervention](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2023/hash/81b8390039b7302c909cb769f8b6cd93-Abstract-Conference.html)
- [arXiv - Grokking as Compression](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.05918)
